name: divider
description: Section boundary slide. Dark canvas with a single heading.
purpose: Marks the start of a major section. Use sparingly — every divider is a context switch for the audience. A 30-slide deck typically has 3-5 dividers; more becomes navigation noise.

eyebrow.description: Optional section number or category label above h1.

whenToUse.title: Major section starts
whenToUse.body: Marks the boundary between two themed sections of the deck. The dark canvas is a strong context-switch signal — use it when the audience needs to re-orient.

whenToUse.title: Sparingly
whenToUse.body: A 30-slide deck typically has 3-5 dividers. More becomes navigation noise; the signal weakens if every third slide is a divider.

whenToUse.title: With an eyebrow
whenToUse.body: An inline-code paragraph above the h1 stamps a section number or category label. Useful for serialized decks where the audience needs to remember which section they're in.

antiPatterns.title: More than five per deck
antiPatterns.body: Each divider is a hard context switch. Too many dilutes the signal and slows the audience. Group related content under fewer sections instead.

antiPatterns.title: Section title that doesn't earn a section
antiPatterns.body: If the next 3-4 slides aren't a coherent unit, a subtopic (lighter, on the bright canvas) is the right tool. Reserve dividers for genuine section starts.

antiPatterns.title: Header or footer overrides
antiPatterns.body: Don't reinstate `_header:` or `_footer:` on a divider. The dark canvas is meant to be uninterrupted; chrome belongs on body slides.

related.name: title
related.when: opens the deck — same dark-bookend chrome

related.name: subtopic
related.when: lighter mid-section orientation on the bright canvas

related.name: closing
related.when: closes the deck — completes the bookend trio

numbered.label: Numbered — auto-incrementing section index
numbered.caption: Stamps an auto-incrementing section number in the corner. Each divider in the deck increments the counter; closing slides carry an independent counter.
